topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my screen, a jarring intrusion into the otherwise calm flow of my afternoon. My thumb hovered over the 'Accept' button, a slow, deliberate movement. It was a profile picture I hadn't seen in fifteen years, a smirk that used to feel like a physical weight on my chest. A cold knot tightened in my stomach. I reread the username—it was him. My breath hitched, a shallow intake of air. Why now? I stared at the screen, my jaw clenching so tightly my molars ached. I quickly minimized the message, shoving my phone into my pocket with unnecessary force. The silence in my room felt suddenly too loud.

[story 2]

I picked up my phone, and the sight of the request made my knuckles whiten around the device. A slow, deep sigh escaped my lips, a sound heavy with exasperation. It was him. Of course, it was him. I felt a distinct tightening across my forehead, a familiar pressure building behind my eyes. I scrolled through my contacts, making sure I didn't accidentally open any old chat logs. The urge to delete the entire application was almost overwhelming. I just wanted this digital intrusion to vanish. I finally tapped 'Decline,' the action feeling disproportionately significant.

[story 3]

The chime of a new connection broke the quiet of my workspace. I didn't look immediately, but I could feel the shift in the air, the subtle tension radiating from the device in my hand. My shoulders tensed up near my ears. I finally glanced down, and the familiar, infuriating avatar swam into view. A slow, almost imperceptible roll of my eyes accompanied the flicker of irritation. I let out a short, sharp exhale, the kind you make when dealing with something utterly tiresome. I deliberately placed the phone face down on the desk, refusing to engage with whatever drama this unsolicited contact represented.
